chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: The group is exploring different facets of the decarbonization challenge, expanding the scope from job transitions to broader issues of science communication and public perception.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Non-speaking participants maintain visual focus on the screen, with Marcel and Ellen providing visible head nods and Marcel offering an audible backchannel.
chunk_summary.artifact_interaction_description: A participant is actively typing notes ('Re-train in new industries', 'Communicate importance') on the shared whiteboard, capturing the spoken ideas in real-time.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group has identified a broad problem area (community engagement and science communication in decarbonization) but has not yet articulated a specific, testable research question.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: Participants are still generating and expanding on broad themes without committing to a specific project direction.
chunk_summary.shared_vision_quote: how do we even get communities to talk about the problem
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group is focusing heavily on the societal and communication aspects of decarbonization rather than technical solutions, indicating a socio-technical approach to the problem space.

ideas_currently_on_table: Job transition and retraining for high-carbon workers
Improving science communication and community engagement regarding decarbonization
chunk_summaries: The group engaged in divergent brainstorming focused on the socio-economic aspects of decarbonization. Julia and Greeshma emphasized the need for job creation and transition pathways for workers in high-carbon industries, while Ellen and Marcel expanded the discussion to address science denial and the need for better communication vehicles to engage communities. The tone was collaborative and supportive, with active note-taking on a shared whiteboard, though no specific research project was crystallized.
